title: Chicken Tikka Masala
time: Prep Time: 25 minutes (plus marination) | Cook Time: 45 minutes

ingredients.title: For the Chicken Tikka Marinade
items: 700 g boneless chicken thighs (skinless, cut into bite-size pieces)
150 g thick yogurt (preferably hung curd or Greek yogurt)
1 ½ tsp Kashmiri red chili powder (for color & mild heat)
1 tsp hot red chili powder (adjust to taste)

ingredients.title: For the Masala (Gravy)
items: 3 tbsp ghee (or neutral oil + 1 tbsp butter)
3 medium ripe tomatoes, pureed (or 250 ml tomato passata)
1 tsp hot chili powder (optional, for heat)
1 tsp kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves, crushed)
½ cup water or stock (adjust consistency)

instruction.title: Step 1: Marinate & Cook the Chicken Tikka
steps: In a bowl, combine all marinade ingredients. Mix well with chicken pieces, ensuring coated evenly.
Cover and marinate at least 2–3 hours (overnight for best results).
Preheat oven or grill to 220°C (425°F). Skewer chicken or place on a wire rack over a tray.
Roast for 12–15 minutes, basting once with oil/butter, until slightly charred and just cooked.
Alternatively, cook in a hot tandoor or grill pan for smokier flavor.

instruction.title: Step 2: Prepare the Masala (Gravy)
steps: Heat ghee + butter in a heavy pan. Add onions and sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste; cook until raw aroma disappears.
Add tomato puree + tomato paste. Cook on medium heat until oil separates and raw taste is gone (8–10 min).
Add chili powders, turmeric, cumin, coriander. Sauté for 1–2 min.
Stir in cream, water/stock, and salt. Simmer gently.
Add roasted chicken tikka pieces, kasuri methi, and garam masala.
Simmer for 8–10 minutes, until chicken absorbs flavors and gravy thickens to silky consistency.

instruction.title: Step 3: Finish & Serve
steps: Garnish with cream swirl & fresh coriander.
Serve hot with butter naan, tandoori roti, or basmati rice.

chefTips: Charred Flavor: For authentic restaurant-style smokiness, briefly expose cooked gravy to charcoal smoke (“dhungar method”) before serving.
Balanced Heat: Use Kashmiri chili for vibrant red color without overwhelming spice.
Texture: Cashew paste (2 tbsp, blended smooth) can be added with cream for a richer, silkier gravy.
Consistency: The sauce should coat the back of a spoon — neither watery nor too thick.
